definition: ngram4's 4-edge path-tuple index makes each step of a multi-step sequential decode path (loop iteration or conjunctive if-cascade) register as new coverage, retaining near-miss inputs that are incrementally progressing through the decode sequence and that single-edge feedback collapses to already-seen buckets and discards.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: ngram4 folds the last 4 edge IDs into the map index, rewarding novel path tuples through the conversion loop and retaining intermediate corpus states that build up surrogate-producing byte arrangements single-edge feedback discards.
signature.one_line: ngram4 path-tuple retention builds surrogate-range code-unit inputs

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must be routed to the UTF16LE decoder and contain a 16-bit code unit whose top six bits equal the 0xD800 high-surrogate pattern.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A byte stream that (a) is detected/handled as UTF-16LE so control reaches UTF16LEToUTF8, and (b) contains a little-endian 16-bit code unit c with (c & 0xFC00) == 0xD800 (a high surrogate, low byte 0x00-0xFF, high byte 0xD8-0xDB), reached while the conversion while-loop is still running.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ngram4 resolved 10/10 trials and drove the branch true 37 times (T=37 vs L T=0), reaching depth-1 lines 534-545 that only the winner explores; the winning seeds carry a UTF-16 BOM-like prefix and 0x00-interleaved bytes (e.g. ff fe ff at 0x0b-0x0d, and 3c 00 3f 00 patterns) that produce surrogate-range code units inside UTF16LEToUTF8.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Plain naive resolved only 1/10, was blocked 8/10 with T=0 F=149 at the blocker and never entered lines 534-545; its single blocking seed is mostly ASCII/0x20 runs that never form a 0xD8xx little-endian code unit.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ngram4 folds the last 4 edge IDs into the map index, so a previously-unseen 4-edge path through the conversion loop registers as new coverage; this finer-grained reward retains the intermediate corpus states that progressively build the surrogate-producing byte arrangement that plain edge coverage saturates on and discards.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the UTF-16LE high-surrogate test in UTF16LEToUTF8.
naive_ngram4 resolved every trial while naive was mostly blocked.
The loser naive resolved only one trial and was blocked in eight.
Only the winner explores the surrogate-decoding lines 534-545 below the blocker.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 534  T=25 F=12  T=0 F=0  if (in >= inend) {           /* handle split mutli-byte c...

evidence_trail.claim: At the blocker the winner takes true 37 times while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 533  T=37 F=735  T=0 F=149  if ((c & 0xFC00) == 0xD800) {    /* surrogates */  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winning seeds carry a UTF-16 byte-order-mark-like prefix and 0x00-interleaved code units.
The loser-blocking seed is dominated by ASCII space runs that cannot form a 0xD8xx code unit.
naive_ngram4 indexes the coverage map by a length-4 edge path so a new path tuple is new coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new
bucket" is a previously-unseen 4-edge path tuple.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile resolved nine trials and reached the highest branch hitcount.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=96  loser=4

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile intercepts integer CMPs into a CMP_MAP keyed by operand-distance bucket.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
each CMP callback writes into a `CMP_MAP` keyed by (PC, operand-distance
bucket)

evidence_trail.claim: A closer-to-matching CMP operand pair adds a CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile uses naive's mutators with no I2S substitution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic UTF16LEToUTF8-style harness gated on (c & 0xFC00)==0xD800 where value_profile and naive_ngram4 resolve at the same rate as plain naive, showing neither CMP_MAP distance feedback nor 4-edge n-gram coverage gives an advantage on this surrogate gate.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Attributing the naive_ngram4 win specifically to 4-edge path coverage (rather than to incidental extra corpus diversity) is the least directly evidenced claim, since no mutation_op lineage was inspected for the n-gram seeds.

signature.mechanism_summary: ngram4's 4-edge path tuples through the multi-step encoding-detection if-cascade yield new-coverage signal at each step, retaining BOM-adjacent near-miss inputs that single-edge feedback collapses to already-seen buckets and discards.
signature.one_line: ngram4 rewards encoding-cascade path tuples, retaining BOM near-miss inputs

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must begin with the exact 3-byte UTF-8 BOM 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF to take the true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same UTF-8 BOM gate (in[0]==0xEF && in[1]==0xBB && in[2]==0xBF), but reached opportunistically: naive_ngram4 needs only the byte triple to appear, with the n-gram-indexed map rewarding the distinct 4-edge path through the BOM-detection cascade.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ngram4 folds the last 4 edge IDs into the map index, so traversing the chain of magic-byte checks in xmlDetectCharEncoding produces previously-unseen 4-edge path tuples that are scored as new coverage, keeping more BOM-adjacent inputs in the corpus; it resolves 9/10 vs naive's 2/10.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: plain naive uses a single-edge MaxMapFeedback, so the intermediate states walking the encoding-detection if-cascade collapse to already-seen edge buckets and are discarded, giving naive no gradient toward the BOM triple (blocked 8/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ngram4's NgramHook indexes the map by length-4 edge-path tuples, so the multi-step encoding-detection cascade yields new-coverage signal that retains more near-miss inputs, whereas naive's single-edge feedback gives no such retention.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is a conjunctive equality test on the first three input bytes against the UTF-8 BOM 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF.
The true side (blocked side T) is the globally blocked side of this branch.
cmplog resolves the branch 10/10 while naive blocks 8/10 with long stall duration and low hitcount.
A winning cmplog seed contains the 0xEF byte that the BOM gate requires.
naive has only havoc and token mutators and no I2S capability.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ngram4 resolves 9/10 versus naive's 2/10 via n-gram coverage feedback.
naive_ngram4 folds a rolling history of the last 4 edge IDs into the map index so a slot encodes a length-4 edge path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
an `NgramHook`
(`HookableInProcessExecutor`) that folds a rolling
history of the last 4 edge IDs into the map index. A map slot
therefore encodes a length-4 edge path (an n-gram of N=4) rather than
a single edge.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ngram4 has no I2S and no CMP_MAP, distinguishing its mechanism from cmplog.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: naive's havoc + token stack. No I2S, no CMP_MAP.

evidence_trail.claim: The loser side (false branch of L987) was taken 39 times by the loser seed set, confirming naive blocks at this gate.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same 3-byte conjunctive magic-byte gate where cmplog fails to resolve the true branch faster than naive (no cmplog advantage), which would refute the I2S-substitution mechanism for this gate.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The naive_ngram4 hypothesis attributes its win to n-gram path retention, but the prompt offers no direct seed-byte evidence that ngram4 specifically retained BOM-adjacent inputs, only the aggregate resolve rate.

signature.mechanism_summary: ngram4's 4-edge path index makes the 3-byte UTF-8 decode block a distinct new bucket, retaining inputs carrying overlong-sequence byte patterns (0xe0 lead + two continuations) that single-edge feedback collapses and discards before val < 0x800 is reachable.
signature.one_line: ngram4 bucket granularity retains overlong-3-byte UTF-8 sequence inputs

analysis.summary_one_line: A 3-byte UTF-8 sequence (lead 0xE0-0xEF + two 0x80-0xBF continuations) decoding to an overlong value < 0x800

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Identical program-side condition: a 3-byte UTF-8 lead byte (c & 0xf0 == 0xe0) with two valid continuation bytes decoding to a value < 0x800 (overlong 3-byte encoding), which takes the true branch (goto encoding_error) at parserInternals.c:622.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ngram4 resolves the branch in 8/10 trials with hitcount 8 on the branch, reaching the same [B]-marked 3-byte decode block and the [W]-marked true side at line 623. All five winner-resolving seeds were stored by naive_ngram4 (e.g. seed id=0020c73cfac74f46, fuzzer=naive_ngram4), and they carry the byte material (e.g. 0xe0 0x99 0x99 runs) that forms 3-byte UTF-8 sequences. The N=4 edge-tuple index turns the path leading into the overlong-3-byte decode into a previously-unseen n-gram, retaining the seed.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks in 8/10 (L T=0 F=1730 at line 622) and stores the loser-blocking seeds (all fuzzer=naive). Its bare per-edge bucket collapses every route into xmlCurrentChar onto the same slots, so an input one mutation away from an overlong-3-byte sequence earns no novelty signal and is dropped before the bytes can be refined to val < 0x800.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ngram4's NgramHook folds a rolling history of the last 4 edge IDs into the map index, so a length-4 edge path through the 3-byte decode block registers as a new bucket; this path-sensitive granularity retains seeds that naive's single-edge feedback discards, supplying the corpus pressure to reach val < 0x800. This is ngram coverage, not comparison-solving.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of `if (val < 0x800)` inside the 3-byte UTF-8 decode block of xmlCurrentChar.
Reaching the 3-byte block requires a 0xE0-class lead byte and valid continuation bytes.
naive_ctx wins pair 1 with ctx_coverage as the technique delta.
naive_ngram4 wins pair 2 with ngram_coverage as the technique delta.
The winner takes the true side of line 622 while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L 622  T=42 F=0  T=0 F=1730  if (val < 0x800)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-resolving seeds were all stored by naive_ngram4 and carry 0xe0 0x99 0x99 byte runs forming 3-byte sequences.
The loser-blocking seeds were all stored by the naive fuzzer.
naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different slots per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ngram4's NgramHook makes a map slot encode a length-4 edge path so a new bucket is a previously-unseen 4-edge path tuple.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
A map slot
therefore encodes a length-4 edge path (an n-gram of N=4) rather than
a single edge.

evidence_trail.claim: Neither involved winner uses I2S or CMP_MAP comparison-solving.
The winner reaches the 3-byte decode block (lines 618-624) which is marked hit-by-both in the per-role overlay.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose decode path to `if (val < 0x800)` is reached from a single fixed call context with no branching edge history would show naive_ctx and naive_ngram4 NOT outperform naive — if they still won there, the divergence is not attributable to coverage-indexing granularity.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The BYTE DIFF section reports no informative W-vs-L byte-level divergence, so the claim that the overlong-3-byte bytes specifically separate winner from loser rests on source structure plus the hit-count split rather than a direct byte contrast.
